name: Code Llama 13B
note: 13B code model with FIM infilling and fill-in-the-middle generation; HumanEval 36.0%.

results.notes: RepoBench v1.1 exact match (EM), averaged across the Python and Java splits reported in Table 17 of the StarCoder2 & The Stack v2 paper (Lozhkov et al., 2024): Python EM 35.50, Java EM 41.27.

HumanEval single-line infilling pass@1, PSM format, long-context-fine-tuned (LCFT) 13B checkpoint — i.e. the actually-released model (matches the file's 16K ctxK). Table 14 (Appendix E) of the Code Llama paper. The paper also reports 80.7 for the pre-LCFT checkpoint and lower multi-line (51.0) and random-span (63.6) infilling scores, which don't map to any taxonomy slug.

pass@1 on the EvalPlus-rigorized HumanEval+ test set (base, non-instruct CodeLlama-13B, greedy decoding), from the official EvalPlus leaderboard results data (evalplus/evalplus.github.io repo, entry 'CodeLlama-13B'). Independent re-evaluation harness, distinct from Meta's self-reported base HumanEval score already tracked.

pass@1 on the EvalPlus-rigorized MBPP+ test set (base, non-instruct CodeLlama-13B, greedy decoding), from the official EvalPlus leaderboard results data (evalplus/evalplus.github.io repo, entry 'CodeLlama-13B').

Overall pass@1 on DS-1000 (mean over 40 samples, temp 0.2/top-p 0.95), Table 11 of the StarCoder2 & The Stack v2 paper (Lozhkov et al., 2024), which independently benchmarks base CodeLlama-13B alongside other open-access code models. Same paper already used in this file for the RepoBench result.

verificationNotes: Confirmed release 2023-08-24 and license. Directly fetched arxiv.org/html/2308.12950v3 Table 2: HumanEval 36.0 / MBPP 47.0 for base Code Llama 13B confirmed exactly. FIM infilling support confirmed for 13B.
